stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 10 observations with mean 45.8 % (within-trial site SD = 7.7). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 51.8 % (within-trial site SD = 3.8). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 54.2 % (within-trial site SD = 7.9). trial site D has 15 observations with mean 56.4 % (within-trial site SD = 6.0). trial site E has 10 observations with mean 54.8 % (within-trial site SD = 3.7). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.1 and 10.2 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 46.6 % (shrunk from raw 45.8). Range [46.2, 47.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 4.1 to 10.2. Pooled mean: 53.1.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.1 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=6.8: B = 0.886.
Interval [46.2, 47.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (45.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.1) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
